description: The ancestral group or ethnicity of the participants. Keywords: Ancestry, Race, Ethnic. Examples: European (EUR), African American (AA), East Asian (EAS), Caribbean Hispanic.
choices: Caucasian: people of European descent or ancestry, commonly referred to as white or European in genetic studies;Multi-ethnic: participants spanning multiple distinct racial or ancestral backgrounds, such as diverse or admixed cohorts;African American: individuals of African descent living in the Americas, including black populations with sub-Saharan African ancestry;Caribbean Hispanic: individuals of Hispanic or Latino origin with roots in the Caribbean region;Asian: individuals of Asian descent, covering East Asian, South Asian, and Southeast Asian populations

description: Study phase indicating whether results come from discovery, replication, meta-analysis, or combined analysis. Keywords: Stage, Phase, Discovery, Replication, Meta, Combined. Examples: Discovery, Replication, Stage 1, Stage 2, Meta-analysis, Joint analysis.
choices: Discovery: the initial phase of a study where associations are first identified in a primary cohort;Replication: a follow-up phase that tests whether associations found in discovery hold in an independent cohort;Validation: a confirmatory phase verifying the robustness of findings, often interchangeable with replication;Joint-analysis: a combined analysis that pools discovery and replication results together;Meta-analysis: a statistical synthesis of results across multiple independent studies;Stage 1: the first stage of a multi-stage study, typically the discovery phase;Stage 2: the second stage of a study, typically the replication or follow-up phase;Stage 3: the third stage of a study, typically a final validation or extension phase

description: The reference panel or method used to infer unobserved genotypes. Keywords: Imputation, Reference panel, Phasing. Examples: 1000 Genomes (1000G), Haplotype Reference Consortium (HRC), TOPMed, UK10K.

Type of genetic data or study design used to generate the results. Keywords: Study type, Genotyping, Sequencing. Examples: GWAS, Whole Exome Sequencing (WES), Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS), Array genotyping.
choices: gene-based: analyses that aggregate variants within a gene to test for association, such as burden or kernel-based tests;SNP-based: analyses that test each individual variant or SNP separately for association

description: The specific trait, disease, or outcome being analyzed. Keywords: Trait, Disease, Outcome. Examples: Alzheimer's disease, BMI, LDL cholesterol, Type 2 diabetes, Blood pressure.

Details of the model that produces the association record. 1) is that a meta-analyses or joint analyses; 2) what kind of regression; 3) what are the covariates; 4) p-value correction method. i.e Inverse-variance weighted Meta-analysis; logistic regression; adjusting for age, sex, first 3 pcs; p value bonferroni correction; other.... Notes: to determine whether it is a meta-analysis, search Meta, Meta-analysis, METAL, inverse-variance weighted, fixed effect
Type of analysis: Age at onset (AAO) / Survival, Cognitive, Cross phenotype, Disease risk, Endophenotype, eQTL, Neuropathology, Pleiotropy
